mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already low, discount rates on future cash flows decline and equity multiples expand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with falling FEDFUNDS do not show higher 20d SPX returns than other periods, or if the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in FEDFUNDS above the neutral range signals policy tightening that compresses valuations and raises financing costs, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances with FEDFUNDS above 2.0% do not have lower 20d SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE captures weakening household income and demand, so a monthly increase in unemployment should be followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces industrial output, demand is outrunning supply, which supports near-term earnings and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds INDPRO growth over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-minus-INDPRO growth spread does not improve 5d SPX returns versus baseline, or if the spread has no incremental predictive power after either series is used alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and spillovers into consumer spending, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month housing declines are not followed by below-median 60d SPX returns, or if the signal's sign reverses in more than one-third of rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A payroll slowdown combined with a rising unemployment rate marks deteriorating labor income growth, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth turns negative and UNRATE rises at least 0.1pp in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-unemployment trigger does not outperform either single proxy alone, or if triggered 20d SPX returns are not negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy rates stay negative and financial conditions remain easy, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if negative real policy rate episodes do not precede stronger 60d SPX returns than positive real-rate episodes, or if the interaction adds no predictive value beyond CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP growth surprise inferred from GDPC1 relative to recent labor-market softness reflects resilient aggregate demand, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is rising while UNRATE stays flat or falls over the same quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with rising GDPC1 and flat/falling UNRATE do not coincide with above-median 60d SPX returns, or if the signal disappears after controlling for GDPC1 alone.
